title: The Textile Playbook
subtitle: How marketing agencies buy branded apparel without losing their minds
market: EU (German pricing benchmarks); frameworks translate globally

lessons.title: The Technique You Pick Determines Your Quality Ceiling
lessons.summary: Embroidery, DTG, screen print, transfer — each technique locks you into specific durability, cost, and visual outcomes.
lessons.takeaway: Match technique to textile and use case before matching to budget.

lessons.title: Why Your "Simple Logo" Quote Varies 300%
lessons.summary: Same design, three suppliers, €800 to €2,400. They're not scamming you — they're pricing different things.
lessons.takeaway: Always ask for the all-in delivered price. Sticker price is fiction.

lessons.title: The Design That Looks Better and Costs Less
lessons.summary: Filled logos cost 3× more and feel like a traffic sign. Outline-instead-of-fill and smarter placement give premium results for half the price.
lessons.takeaway: Ask every supplier: "What's the most cost-effective way to achieve this look?"
sections: Real example: full-back vs chest logo

lessons.title: The Three Questions That Expose Bad Suppliers
lessons.summary: Most suppliers aren't scammers — they're disorganized. These three questions expose where process breaks down.
lessons.takeaway: Named contact + proof process + per-piece QC = operational foundation.

lessons.title: When Cheap Becomes Expensive
lessons.summary: The €3 cap quote looks like a win until you're explaining to your client why their logo is peeling after three washes.
lessons.takeaway: Price × Risk Factor = True Cost. Client-facing work is ×2 by default.

lessons.title: Timeline Management Without Panic
lessons.summary: Rush fees, realistic deadlines, and the seasonal chaos that turns "2 weeks" into "next month."
lessons.takeaway: The rush fee isn't the problem. The lack of buffer is.
sections: Pay vs push back on client

lessons.title: The Prototype Investment
lessons.summary: Sampling isn't optional for anything that matters. A €30 prototype eliminates 80% of pre-production risk.
lessons.takeaway: €30 prototype on a €2,000 order is insurance, not overhead.

lessons.title: When Things Go Wrong
lessons.summary: Error happened. Client is angry. Supplier is defensive. How to fix without breaking the relationship.
lessons.takeaway: A supplier's response to the first mistake is your real contract.

lessons.title: Pricing Benchmarks That Actually Exist
lessons.summary: Real numbers from the German/EU textile market — embroidery, screen print, DTF per-piece costs and where suppliers make their margin.
lessons.takeaway: Walk into negotiations with ranges, not asks. "I'm seeing €X–€Y" beats "Can you do €X?"

rules: Always ask for the all-in delivered price
PDF proof for digital approval; physical prototype for production go-ahead

ai_guidance.citation_format: From The Textile Playbook (CC BY-SA 4.0, textileplaybook.com)
good_uses: Answer specific questions about EU branded apparel sourcing
Generate supplier vetting scripts based on the rubric
limitations: Pricing is EU/Germany snapshot — adjust for other markets
Frameworks evergreen; specific numbers may shift quarterly
